news_articles.title: Solana (SOL) Price USD Today, News, Charts, Market Cap | Coinbase
news_articles.content: Solana reached its highest recorded price in January 2025, when it peaked at $293.31. This reflected a period of increased network activity and adoption. Since then, the price has fluctuated based on ecosystem developments and market conditions. Tracking Solana’s live price can provide insights into its ongoing performance. [...] Solana is used in a wide range of blockchain applications, from decentralized finance (DeFi) to NFTs and blockchain-based games. The network's infrastructure aims to support the execution of smart contracts, tokenized assets, real-time transactions, and decentralized exchanges. Projects built on Solana include tools for lending, staking, synthetic assets, and automated market makers. Because of its speed and relatively low transaction costs, Solana has attracted developers seeking alternatives to other high-fee networks. The growing adoption of the Solana blockchain contributes to user interest, which may affect the live Solana price and trading volume. [...] Solana was conceptualized in 2017 by Anatoly Yakovenko, a former engineer with a background in distributed systems. Along with Greg Fitzgerald and others, Yakovenko developed a new blockchain protocol that introduced proof-of-history (PoH) as a key innovation. Solana Labs was formed to lead the development of the network, which officially launched its mainnet in 2020. Since launch, Solana has grown to host a wide range of dApps and has seen increasing usage across the NFT and DeFi sectors. Its native asset, SOL, is used across the Solana ecosystem for transactions and network participation. Interest in Solana's price has grown alongside its user adoption and the expansion of blockchain-based use cases.

news_articles.title: Solana (SOL) News Today | CoinDesk
news_articles.content:
# Solana (SOL) News Today | CoinDesk

Search

/

 was launched in 2020 by Anatoly Yakovenko as a high-performance blockchain designed to support thousands of transactions per second through its innovative Proof of History consensus mechanism combined with Proof of Stake. Built to compete directly with Ethereum, Solana offers developers fast transaction speeds and low fees, making it attractive for DeFi applications, NFT marketplaces, and Web3 gaming platforms. [...] The Solana ecosystem has rapidly expanded to include major DeFi protocols like Serum and Raydium, popular NFT collections, and gaming projects that leverage the network's speed and cost advantages. Despite experiencing several network outages that raised questions about decentralization and stability, Solana has maintained strong developer activity and institutional backing, with SOL serving as both a utility token for transaction fees and a staking asset for network security.

news_articles.title: Latest Solana News - (SOL) Future Outlook, Trends & Market Insights
news_articles.content:
What this means: This is bullish for SOL because drastically lower compute costs for tokens can trigger a "rebirth of DeFi" on Solana, enabling more sophisticated applications and improving the user experience through lower fees. Its backward-compatible design minimizes disruption for existing projects.

## Conclusion

Solana's 2026 roadmap is strategically focused on deepening its technical moat: Alpenglow chases ultimate speed, Firedancer fortifies infrastructure, and efficient tokens unlock new economic design space. The combined trajectory aims to transition Solana from a high-performance chain to the settled layer for global internet capital markets. Will these upgrades be enough to catalyze the next wave of institutional adoption?

CMC AI can make mistakes. Not financial advice. [...] ### 3. @jackieeboy17: Eyes Range Breakout mixed

"Solana has been accumulating within $78-90 range... very close to a potential breakout... If we get a high volume breakout, the first major target... is $110." – @jackieeboy17 (6.4k followers · Impressions not specified · 2026-03-23 13:13 UTC) View original postWhat this means: This is neutral for SOL, framing the current price action as a coiled spring. The next sustained move above $90 or below $78 on high volume will likely determine the short-to-medium-term trend.

## Conclusion [...] ## Conclusion

Solana's development trajectory balances critical maintenance with transformative scalability leaps, from updating core libraries to fundamentally re-engineering its consensus for speed. How will the rollout of Alpenglow's sub-second finality reshape competitive dynamics among Layer 1 blockchains?

## What is next on SOL’s roadmap?

TLDR

Solana's development continues with these milestones:

1.   Alpenglow Consensus Upgrade (Q2–Q3 2026) – Aims for sub-second transaction finality by overhauling the network's core consensus logic.

2.   Firedancer Validator Client Optimizations (2026) – Ongoing enhancements to the independent client to boost network throughput and resilience.

news_articles.title: Latest ##solana News, Opinions and Feed Today
news_articles.content: 2020: $SOL was very cheap, mostly under $2. Few people knew about it. 2021: Huge bull run. Solana exploded to around $260 at its peak because of NFTs and meme hype. 2022: Big crash after the crypto bear market and the FTX collapse. $SOL dropped below $10. 2023: Recovery year. Developers stayed active and price slowly came back above $100. 2024: Strong growth again with meme coins, DeFi, and fast transactions bringing users back. 2025: Many analysts expect Solana to stay one of the top altcoins. Possible range around $180–$400 if the market stays bullish. 2026: Bullish forecasts say $SOL could reach $300–$500+ if crypto adoption and ETF interest continue growing. Bad market conditions could still push it much lower. #Trump'sIranAttackDelayed #solana #TradingCommunity {future}(SOLUSDT) [...] Solana Under Pressure at $84: Technical Trap or Mega Discount Zone? ⚡ ​The Analysis: Solana ($SOL {spot}(SOLUSDT) ) is testing the nerves of aggressive bulls, currently hovering right around the $84 mark. Price is heavily compressing against the lower rising channel rail that has supported the ecosystem since its February swing lows. ​The Alpha: Funding rates on perpetual futures have taken a fascinating turn, flipping negative to -3% as short exposure builds up. This means shorters are actively paying longs to keep their positions open. Furthermore, a heavy overhead ceiling sits at $87.80 (20-day EMA) and $87.60 (50-day EMA). ​The Trade: If the current channel structure fractures, look out below—there is a high-probability liquidity sweep zone waiting between $76 and $78. However, if [...] sweep zone waiting between $76 and $78. However, if buyers step in to force funding back to neutral, a squeeze back over $87.80 targets the major rejection supply zone at $96–$98. ​Funding is negative and shorts are crowded. Is a massive short-squeeze about to ignite? 👇 #sol #solana #cryptosignals #defi
